annexTitle: Annex B — Implementation Guidance for AI Controls
description: Annex B provides guidance on implementing the controls listed in Annex A. Organized to correspond to each Annex A control domain.

guidanceAreas.title: Policies Related to AI
guidanceAreas.guidance: AI policy should address: scope of AI activities, risk appetite for AI, ethical principles, compliance requirements, roles and responsibilities, and review mechanisms. Policy should be communicated to all relevant personnel.

guidanceAreas.title: Internal Organization
guidanceAreas.guidance: Organizations should establish clear governance structures for AI including: designated AI oversight roles, cross-functional teams for AI risk assessment, documented processes for impact assessment, and stakeholder engagement mechanisms.

guidanceAreas.title: Resources for AI Systems
guidanceAreas.guidance: Transparency measures should be proportionate to the AI system's impact. Explainability techniques should be selected based on the system's complexity, risk level, and audience. Accessibility should follow universal design principles.

guidanceAreas.title: AI System Lifecycle
guidanceAreas.guidance: AI system development should follow structured processes with clear gates for requirements review, design review, verification, validation, deployment approval, and decommissioning. Documentation should be maintained throughout.

guidanceAreas.title: Data for AI Systems
guidanceAreas.guidance: Data governance should address: quality metrics and thresholds, provenance tracking, bias identification and mitigation, privacy impact assessment, and data lifecycle management including secure disposal.

guidanceAreas.title: Information Security for AI Systems
guidanceAreas.guidance: AI-specific security considerations include: adversarial attack resilience, model integrity protection, data poisoning prevention, prompt injection defence (for GenAI), and supply chain security for pre-trained models.

guidanceAreas.title: AI System Monitoring
guidanceAreas.guidance: Monitoring should cover: model performance metrics, data drift detection, fairness metrics over time, impact indicators, and incident detection. Automated alerting for out-of-bounds performance is recommended.

guidanceAreas.title: Third-Party and Customer Relationships
guidanceAreas.guidance: Third-party risk management for AI should include: due diligence on AI component providers, contractual requirements for AI quality and ethics, and ongoing monitoring of third-party AI component performance.
